verses.1: Do not boast about tomorrow, For you do not know what a day may bring.
verses.2: Let another praise you, and not your own mouth; A stranger, and not your own lips.
verses.3: A stone is heavy and the sand weighty, But the provocation of a fool is heavier than both of them.
verses.4: Wrath is fierce and anger is a flood, But who can stand before jealousy?
verses.5: Better is open rebuke Than love that is concealed.
verses.6: Faithful are the wounds of a friend, But deceitful are the kisses of an enemy.
verses.7: A satisfied person despises honey, But to a hungry person any bitter thing is sweet.
verses.8: Like a bird that wanders from its nest, So is a person who wanders from his home.
verses.9: Oil and perfume make the heart glad, And a person’s advice is sweet to his friend.
verses.10: Do not abandon your friend or your father’s friend, And do not go to your brother’s house on the day of your disaster; Better is a neighbor who is near than a brother far away.
verses.11: Be wise, my son, and make my heart glad, So that I may reply to one who taunts me.
verses.12: A prudent person sees evil and hides himself; But the naive proceed, and pay the penalty.
verses.13: Take his garment when he becomes a guarantor for a stranger; And for a foreign woman seize a pledge from him.
verses.14: One who blesses his friend with a loud voice early in the morning, It will be considered a curse to him.
verses.15: A constant dripping on a day of steady rain And a contentious woman are alike;
verses.16: He who would restrain her restrains the wind, And grasps oil with his right hand.
verses.17: As iron sharpens iron, So one person sharpens another.
verses.18: One who tends the fig tree will eat its fruit, And one who cares for his master will be honored.
verses.19: As in water a face reflects the face, So the heart of a person reflects the person.
verses.20: Sheol and Abaddon are never satisfied, Nor are the eyes of a person ever satisfied.
verses.21: The crucible is for silver and the furnace for gold, And each is tested by the praise accorded him.
verses.22: Though you pound the fool in a mortar with a pestle along with crushed grain, His foolishness still will not leave him.
verses.23: Know well the condition of your flocks, And pay attention to your herds;
verses.24: For riches are not forever, Nor does a crown endure to all generations.
verses.25: When the grass disappears, the new growth is seen, And the herbs of the mountains are gathered in,
verses.26: The lambs will be for your clothing, And the goats will bring the price of a field,
verses.27: And there will be enough goats’ milk for your food, For the food of your household, And sustenance for your attendants.
